MSFT.reasoning: Branch B (buy the dip in an uptrend) fires with moderate conviction. Branch A (fade the extreme) does NOT fire — daily Z-score is only 0.60 (well within ±1), BB %B at 0.55 is mid-band, and RSI-14 at 64.93 does not reach the >75 overbought threshold. For Branch B: the dominant uptrend is confirmed by EMA-aligned-uptrend=true, price above SMA-50 (+3.0%), and the wiki records the SuperTrend flip on April 15 as the key structural shift. MSFT pulled back ~6.4% from the recent swing high of 433.70 to the April 30 intraday low of 398.01, nearly tagging the 50% Fibonacci level (394.99) before recovering. The current hourly price of ~415.74 is sitting between the daily 23.6% Fib (415.43) and the daily 38.2% Fib (404.13). Key caution: April 30 volume was 2.06x the 20-day average — expanding volume on a pullback is a Branch B warning per strategy rules, and hourly indicators still show a bearish bias (hourly ADX -DI 38.88 >> +DI 20.38, hourly MACD negative at -1.72). However, hourly RSI-21 at 39.01 is approaching oversold, hourly OBV is trending up (bullish divergence from price), and the intraday bounce from 398.01 signals that buyers stepped in at the 50% Fib area. The confluence of the daily 38.2% Fib (404.13) with the horizontal support cluster near 404-407 (prior pivot behavior and 21-day SMA 401.90) forms a reasonable re-entry zone if price pulls back again; otherwise the current bounce level (415-416) offers a tighter risk entry with a stop just below the 50% Fib at 394.99. Target is the re-test of the recent swing high area / hourly resistance at 429.91 (14 tests) and then the full swing high of 433.70. Confidence capped at 58 due to the high volume on the down day and unresolved hourly bearish momentum — this is a buy-the-dip setup, not a confirmed reversal candle.
